issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn` includes a `transition` that animates `transform`, and the `:active` state moves the button with `transform: translateY(1px)`. No `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disables this motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who reduce motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover` applies `transform: translateY(-1px)` while inheriting the `.btn` transition on `transform`. No reduced‑motion media query disables this hover movement, breaching WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Product count badge updates are not announced to screen‑reader users
issuesFound.explanation: When the product grid is filtered the visible count is changed by updating the text of the element with `data-product-count`, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new total. Users of assistive technology will not know that the result set has changed.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter “chips” UI is not announced when added or removed
issuesFound.explanation: The chip list reflects the current filter state, but the script never sends a live‑region announcement when a chip is added or removed. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive that a filter has been applied or cleared.

issuesFound.title: Search‑suggestion list changes are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the user types a query the suggestion panel is populated, but the code never announces how many suggestions were returned. Users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the list has updated.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter‑form email validation does not set `aria-invalid` on error
issuesFound.explanation: The email field is validated with `validateField`, but when the value is invalid the script only shows a toast‑style message; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` (or removes it on success). Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑navigation overlay hidden state is not conveyed with `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: When the off‑canvas navigation overlay is closed the script adds the `hidden` attribute but never sets `aria-hidden="true"`. Screen‑reader users may still perceive the overlay as part of the page.

issuesFound.title: “No results” empty‑state for the product grid is hidden without announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter yields zero products the empty‑state element is shown, but no live‑region message is emitted. Users of assistive technology are left unaware that the filter produced no matches.

issuesFound.title: File‑upload component error messages are not linked via `aria-describedby`
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation the script creates a status `<span>` but never associates it with the `<input type="file">` using `aria-describedby`. Screen‑reader users cannot hear the validation message.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown‑menu panels lack an explicit `role="menu"` or `role="listbox"` when used as navigation menus
issuesFound.explanation: The script only ensures the panel is hidden/shown but does not enforce a semantic ARIA role. If the markup omits `role="menu"` or `role="listbox"` the menu may be announced as a generic region, reducing accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Search‑results highlighter does not announce when highlights are applied
issuesFound.explanation: After injecting `<mark>` elements the function updates the live region with a generic message, but the message does not include the search term, making it unclear what was highlighted.
issuesFound.fix:
Include the query in the announcement:  

```js
liveRegion.announce(
  `${_marks.length} matches highlighted for “${query}”`);
```

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) is given `aria-expanded` but does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the navigation drawer element. Without this relationship screen‑reader users cannot discover which region the button controls.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field not reflected in aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit` the email address is validated manually, but when the validation fails the input is not marked with `aria-invalid="true"`. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error state.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge (`[data-product-count]`) is updated, but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new total to screen‑reader users, leaving them unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a filter‑chip removes the corresponding filter, but the UI does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users of assistive technology do not know the filter was cleared.
issuesFound.fix:
After removing a chip, announce the action.  
  ```js
  liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed`);
  ```

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden state lacks aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll, the script adds a CSS class (`headerHidden`) but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. The element remains perceivable to screen readers, causing confusion.

issuesFound.title: Empty‑state element visibility change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the current filters, the empty‑state message (`[data-empty-state]`) is shown, but there is no ARIA live announcement, so users are not informed that the result set is empty.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation overlay hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element (`.nav-overlay`) is hidden by adding a CSS class and the `hidden` attribute, but `aria-hidden` is never set, leaving the overlay in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the script removes the `open` class and later adds `hidden`, but it never updates `aria-hidden`. The panel remains announced by screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel show does not clear aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the script removes the `hidden` attribute but does not clear `aria-hidden`, so the panel stays hidden from assistive technology even though it is visible.

issuesFound.title: Theme‑switcher toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The theme‑switcher UI uses buttons with `data-theme-toggle` to cycle themes, but the script only updates `aria-pressed` on buttons that match the current theme. Buttons that are not the current theme retain no explicit `aria-pressed` state, which can be ambiguous for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the off‑canvas drawer but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the hamburger button. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the navigation is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle() handler changes the drawer’s visibility but does not reflect the button’s pressed state via aria‑pressed. Assistive technologies cannot convey the toggle’s on/off status.
issuesFound.fix:
After calling open() or close(), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));` so the button’s aria‑pressed matches its state.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() removes hidden from the panel and adds a CSS class, yet never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button. The expanded state is invisible to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when the panel is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: In _dropdownMenu.register() the code only adds aria‑controls if the panel already has an id. Many dropdown panels lack an id, leaving the trigger without a reference to the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each panel receives a generated id (e.g., via srUtils.ensureId(panel) ) and always set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panel.id);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open/close functions update visibility but never write to a live region, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` and after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` or use a dedicated aria‑live status element.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” does not set aria‑expanded to true on the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel but never updates the trigger’s aria‑expanded attribute, leaving the state hidden from AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when the item is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds error text and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the offending input, nor removes it on success. Assistive technologies cannot detect validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
In the if (!result.valid) branch, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` and in the else branch, `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips UI updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: activeFilterChips.render() adds or removes chip elements without any aria‑live announcement, so users of screen readers are unaware that filters have changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After rendering, update a hidden <div role="status" aria‑live="polite"> with a message such as `“Filters applied: ${list of active filters}”`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” does not set aria‑pressed to false on the toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes the drawer but never updates aria‑pressed on the hamburger button, leaving its pressed state stale for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` when the drawer is closed.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “close” does not set aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button (duplicate of Issue 6 but for the close path)
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.close() hides the panel but does not reset aria‑expanded to false on the trigger, so the button incorrectly reports an expanded state after being collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function shows the mobile navigation drawer but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑pressed handling
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) toggles the drawer but never updates `aria‑pressed`, which is required for a button that represents a two‑state toggle.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened, the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute remains `"false"`; the attribute is only set to `"false"` on close.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown updates the UI but does not provide an ARIA live‑region announcement, so users are not informed that the menu has opened.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria‑expanded true on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion’s `open()` function expands the answer panel but never updates `aria-expanded="true"` on the question button, breaking the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer remains in accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by CSS transforms only; no `aria-hidden` is applied when the drawer is closed, so assistive technologies still expose its content.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria‑invalid on validation errors
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` helper only adds error text and CSS classes; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge updates are not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the visible product count changes, the UI updates the badge text but does not announce the new count via a live region, leaving users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing savings message updates lack live‑region support
issuesFound.explanation: The element `_savingsMsg` shows discount information but has no `aria-live` attribute, so changes are not announced to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panel not marked aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function hides the dropdown panel via CSS classes but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, leaving the hidden panel exposed to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes and shows the drawer but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may be missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In mobileNav.init() the code only adds aria‑controls to the trigger if the drawer element already has an id. If the drawer lacks an id (the common case), the trigger has no aria‑controls, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer receives a unique id (e.g., setAttr(_drawer, 'id', 'mobile-nav-drawer');) and always set aria‑controls on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the open CSS class and shows the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, so assistive technologies cannot detect the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Call setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded to false
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() removes the open class but only sets aria‑expanded to false when called via _escape handling; other close paths (e.g., clicking outside) leave aria‑expanded stale.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); is executed in every close path.

issuesFound.title: Product filter does not announce result count changes via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters() updates the visible cards and count badge but never announces the new count to screen‑reader users, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count badge, call liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count!==1?'s':''} shown`); or similar.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult() adds error text and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on the field, so ATs cannot convey the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In _ renderResult when result.valid is false, call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); (and remove it when valid).

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set aria‑invalid on fields with errors
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form uses inlineValidator.validate() which suffers from the same missing aria‑invalid issue, leaving form errors invisible to assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Apply the same fix as Issue 6 to the contact form fields (ensure aria‑invalid is set/cleared).

issuesFound.title: Discount code input does not set aria‑invalid when an invalid code is entered
issuesFound.explanation: discountCodeInput._onApply() displays an error message but never marks the input as aria‑invalid, so screen‑reader users cannot detect the problem.
issuesFound.fix: When result.valid is false, call setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true'); and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑controls linking to the price display panel
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (data‑period) control the price display but do not have aria‑controls pointing to the element that updates, breaking the control‑panel relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Assign each button an aria‑controls attribute referencing the id of the price container (e.g., setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'price-panel')).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel hide does not set aria‑hidden when hidden via CSS class only
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() removes the open class and adds hidden attribute, but if the panel is hidden only by removing a CSS class (e.g., CSS.open) without the hidden attribute, aria‑hidden remains false, leaving the panel in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure that any hide operation also sets setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); (and removes it on show).

fpTitles: Mobile navigation trigger may be missing aria‑controls attribute

Discount code input does not set aria‑invalid when an invalid code is entered

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown menu (registered in `dropdownMenu.register`) toggles the panel visibility via `_openInstance` / `_closeInstance` but never sets or updates the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger element. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open` the FAQ item’s trigger button is not given `aria-expanded="true"` when the answer panel is shown. The close function does set it to false, so the open state is never announced.
issuesFound.fix: Call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` at the start of the `open` function.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email field is validated with `inlineValidator.bind`, but `inlineValidator` only adds CSS error classes; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` on error or removes it on success. Assistive technologies therefore do not receive error state.
issuesFound.fix: Extend the inline‑validation render step to call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` on error and `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid')` on success.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation does not set aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: All contact‑form fields are bound with `inlineValidator.bind`, yet the validator does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid inputs. Errors are only shown visually.
issuesFound.fix: In the validation render, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')` for invalid fields and clear the attribute when the field becomes valid.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide uses off‑screen technique without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll (`stickyHeader.hide`), it is moved off‑screen via CSS (`addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden)`) but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`. The element remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: In `hide()`, add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `show()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not set aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` removes the open class from the drawer but does not set `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element, leaving it perceivable to AT even when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After removing the open class, call `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu emits custom events but does not provide an audible announcement (no `aria-live` update). Users of assistive tech are unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? 'Menu opened' : 'Menu closed')` or similar.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing a FAQ item triggers custom events but does not update any live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback about the change.
issuesFound.fix: After `open(item)` and `close(item)`, call `liveRegion.announce(item.open ? 'Answer expanded' : 'Answer collapsed')`.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide does not remove from accessibility tree (duplicate check)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 5 – the hidden header remains in the accessibility tree because `aria-hidden` is never set.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 5.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not remove from accessibility tree (duplicate check)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 6 – the drawer stays in the accessibility tree after being hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 6.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is initialized, the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` but its `aria-expanded` attribute is set to `"false"` only if the attribute is missing; however, the registration code does not guarantee the attribute exists for every trigger, leaving some triggers without an explicit collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` is always called during registration.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing aria‑expanded on initial load
issuesFound.explanation: During FAQ parsing, the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is only set based on existing markup; if absent, the trigger starts without an explicit collapsed state, which is ambiguous for AT.
issuesFound.fix: After parsing, call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` for all items that are not initially open.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer hide does not set aria‑hidden
Sticky header hide does not remove from accessibility tree (duplicate check)
Mobile navigation drawer hide does not remove from accessibility tree (duplicate check)

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function (and its open/close helpers) change the navigation drawer’s visibility but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button (`.nav-toggle`). Keyboard users and AT cannot know the drawer’s state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: dropdownMenu._openInstance adds hidden to the panel and adds a CSS class, but never updates aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
In _openInstance and _closeInstance add:

    setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');   // on open
    setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');  // on close

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions list updated without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: siteSearch._fetchAndRender updates the suggestion list via suggestionListRenderer.render but does not announce the new results to a screen‑reader. Users won’t know that suggestions have changed.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering, call liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} suggestion${results.length===1?'':'s'} found`); or a similar message.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count badge changes without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter._updateCountBadge updates the numeric badge but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users of assistive tech are not informed when the number of visible products changes.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the badge text, add:

    liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`);

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator.validate marks fields as invalid by adding error text, but never sets aria‑invalid="true" (or removes it on valid). AT therefore cannot detect validation errors.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle button missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button used by mobileNav does not have an aria‑expanded attribute in the initial markup, so its collapsed state is unknown to assistive technology on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.init after locating _trigger, set:

    setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons lack an aria‑expanded attribute in the initial DOM, leaving their collapsed state ambiguous.
issuesFound.fix:
In dropdownMenu.init or when registering each trigger, add:

    setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter UI changes (view mode toggle) not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the view mode (grid ↔ list) updates classes but does not announce the change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
After toggling classes, call:

    liveRegion.announce(`View mode switched to ${mode} view`);

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show not announced
issuesFound.explanation: stickyHeader.hide and show add/remove CSS classes but do not provide any ARIA live feedback, so users cannot know when the header disappears or reappears.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period switch (`[data-billing-toggle]`) toggles visual state but does not expose its on/off state via aria‑pressed, making its state invisible to AT.

fpTitles: Sticky header hide/show not announced

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button (`#nav-menu > li > button[aria-haspopup]`) toggles its panel open/closed via `openInstance`/`closeInstance`, but the code never updates `aria-expanded` on the button. Screen‑reader users cannot know the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: In `openInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true')`; in `closeInstance` add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing aria‑expanded true on open
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open(item)` the panel is shown and `item.open` set, but the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"` (only set to `"false"` on close). Assistive technologies cannot detect that the answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true')` when opening the item.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion dynamic changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item updates visibility but does not provide an audible announcement. WCAG 4.1.3 requires that such state changes be announced to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question))`; after closing, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question))`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing aria‑controls on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The same hamburger button controls the drawer (`#nav-drawer`), yet no `aria-controls` attribute is set to reference the drawer’s ID. This breaks the relationship required for screen‑reader navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has an ID (e.g., `id="nav-drawer"`), then in `mobileNav.init()` add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, 'nav-drawer')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed (`mobileNav.close()`), the script removes CSS classes but never sets `aria-hidden="true"` on the drawer element. The drawer remains focusable and announced by AT even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.close()` after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, 'true')`; in `mobileNav.open()` remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button `.nav-toggle` opens and closes the navigation drawer but never sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')` when opening and `setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')` when closing, e.g.:  
    Line 845: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true');`  
    Line 861: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The same button acts as a two‑state toggle but never reflects its state with `aria‑pressed`, so AT cannot tell whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix:
Update `aria‑pressed` alongside `aria‑expanded`:  
    Line 845: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-pressed','true');`  
    Line 861: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-pressed','false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The button does not reference the drawer it controls, breaking the required relationship for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Give the drawer an `id` and reference it:  
    Line 842: `_drawer.id = 'nav-drawer';`  
    Line 842: `_trigger.setAttribute('aria-controls','nav-drawer');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons that open/close a dropdown never set `aria-expanded` on the trigger, so the open/closed state is invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true')`; in _closeInstance add `trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set aria‑expanded to true on open
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item expands, AT still reads the button as collapsed because `aria-expanded` remains false.
issuesFound.fix:
Add at the start of open():  
    Line 1912: `item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded','true');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing a FAQ item changes visible content but no `aria-live` announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After the state change, announce the action:  
    Line 1912: `liveRegion.announce('Answer expanded');`  
    Line 1940: `liveRegion.announce('Answer collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, users of assistive technology do not hear how many products are shown.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting the badge text, add:  
    Line 1584: `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden with CSS only, without aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible, causing confusion for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In close():  
    Line 861: `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden','true');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is shown without removing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: AT still treats the drawer as hidden even though it is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In open():  
    Line 845: `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed and aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: The switch that changes billing period does not expose its state or the region it controls, violating ARIA button‑toggle patterns.
issuesFound.fix:
After locating `_toggleEl`:  
    Line 2260: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed','false');`  
    Line 2260: `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-controls','pricing-period-panel');`

issuesFound.title: Changing billing period does not announce the new price to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Users of assistive technology are not informed that the pricing has switched (e.g., from monthly to annual).
issuesFound.fix:
At the end of setPeriod():  
    Line 2298: `liveRegion.announce('Pricing switched to '+period+' billing');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens or closes, the visual change is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Add announcements:  
    Line 1065: `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`  
    Line 1075: `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');`

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer is shown without removing aria‑hidden

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not set aria‑expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` function shows the navigation drawer but never updates the hamburger button’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.open` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `mobileNav.close` add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls linking to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) has no `aria-controls` attribute that references the drawer element, breaking the relationship required for ARIA disclosure.
issuesFound.fix: Give the drawer an id (e.g., `nav-drawer`) and in `mobileNav.init` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'nav-drawer');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region announcement is made when the navigation menu is opened or closed, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (status messages).
issuesFound.fix: After opening call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen);` and after closing call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not set aria‑expanded false on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `mobileNav.close` hides the drawer but leaves the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute unchanged, leaving AT with stale state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside `mobileNav.close`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance` makes a dropdown panel visible but never updates the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close does not set aria‑expanded false on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance` hides a dropdown panel but does not reset `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving it permanently “true”.
issuesFound.fix: Inside `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: No live‑region message is emitted when a dropdown opens or closes, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After opening call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened);` and after closing call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed);`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded on question button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open` expands an answer panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the associated trigger button, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion close does not set aria‑expanded false on question button
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.close` collapses an answer panel but leaves `aria‑expanded` on the trigger set to “true”.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate` only adds visual error messages; it never adds `aria-invalid="true"` to the failing field, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In `validate()` when `result.valid` is false, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously invalid field becomes valid, the code removes the error message but leaves `aria-invalid="true"` on the field, causing a false error indication.
issuesFound.fix: When `result.valid` is true, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce new period to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod` updates UI and price values but does not provide a live‑region announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the billing‑period switch.
issuesFound.fix: After the UI update, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged.replace('{period}', BILLING_PERIODS[period].label));`.

issuesFound.title: Site‑search status element is hidden and lacks role = status, preventing announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The status element used for search result counts is created with `hidden` and without an explicit `role="status"`, so live‑region updates are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure `_statusEl` is not hidden (remove `hidden` attribute) and set `setAttr(_statusEl, 'role', 'status');` when creating it.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle function does not update aria‑expanded

issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening and `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In the `register` function of the dropdown menu, the trigger button is given `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to assistive technologies on page load.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-controls`, also call `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle` function toggles the drawer but never updates `aria-pressed` on the hamburger button, so the pressed state is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` and in `close()` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: During `mobileNav.init` the trigger receives `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not exposed to AT on load.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after setting `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`_onPeriodBtnClick`) change the billing period but never set `aria-pressed` on the buttons, so the selected period is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the period, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btnPeriod === _pricingState.period ? 'true' : 'false');` for each button.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button missing initial aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: In `pricing.init` the period buttons are not given an initial `aria-pressed` state, leaving the default selection ambiguous to AT.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the buttons, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', btn.getAttribute('data-period') === _pricingState.period ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in `faqAccordion` change panel visibility but never announce the state change via a live region, so screen‑reader users are not informed when an answer expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened || 'Answer expanded');` and after closing, announce a collapse message.

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger button missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqRegistry.parse` the trigger receives `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed on load.
issuesFound.fix: After setting `aria-controls`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions emit custom events but do not write to any `aria-live` region, leaving users unaware of menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, update a hidden live region (e.g., `_statusEl.textContent = 'Menu opened';`) and similarly for closing.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions of `mobileNav` emit custom events but do not provide an audible announcement of the navigation drawer state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region (e.g., `
`) and set its text to “Navigation menu opened” or “Navigation menu closed” inside the respective functions.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher button does not update aria‑pressed consistently
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button updates the visual state but only sets `aria-pressed` on click; it does not synchronize the attribute when the theme is changed programmatically (e.g., on page load), causing a mismatch for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `themeSwitcher.set`, after applying the theme, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === pref));` for all theme buttons.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher button missing initial aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the theme buttons are not given an initial `aria-pressed` value, so the current theme is not announced.
issuesFound.fix: After determining the stored theme, iterate over `[data-theme-toggle]` buttons and set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === storedPref));`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input references non‑existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for the newsletter email includes `aria-describedby='newsletter-email-error'`, but no element with that id is rendered, so screen readers cannot announce the validation error. This affects users who rely on ARIA live regions for form feedback.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” input references missing error element
issuesFound.explanation: The `<TextInput>` for “Full name” has `aria-describedby='contact-name-error'`, but no element with that id is rendered, preventing error announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass the error id and message to `<FormGroup>` so it renders the `<ErrorMessage>` with the correct id.  
  ```tsx
  // Line 1248: modify the FormGroup usage
  <FormGroup
  ```

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input references missing error element

  ```tsx

      id="contact-email"
      name="email"
      type="email"
      autoComplete="email"
      required
      aria-required="true"
      value={email.state.value}
      onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
      onBlur={email.onBlur}
      invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
      aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined}
    />
  </FormGroup>
  ```

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form submit button does not expose loading state to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` is passed a prop `loading={isSubmitting}`, but the `Button` component expects `isLoading`. Consequently `aria-busy` is never set, so assistive technologies cannot detect the loading state. This affects users who need to know when the form is processing.

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button does not expose loading state to AT
issuesFound.explanation: The `<Button>` receives `loading={isSubmitting}` instead of the expected `isLoading`, so `aria-busy` is never applied and the loading state is invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Modal backdrop missing button semantics and keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>` is clickable to close the dialog but lacks `role="button"`, a focusable `tabIndex`, and keyboard handling (Enter/Space). Keyboard‑only users cannot activate the backdrop to dismiss the modal, violating WCAG 2.2 §4.1.2.

issuesFound.explanation: The email field is wrapped in FormGroup which renders a <label without htmlFor, so the input is not programmatically associated with its label, breaking label‑for relationship.
issuesFound.fix:
In FormGroup component (line ≈ 1150), add htmlFor to the label element:

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email <input> missing aria‑invalid when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the email input is invalid, the component does not set aria‑invalid, so AT is not informed of the error state.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email <input> error message not linked via aria‑describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The input’s aria‑describedby references an ID that is never rendered because the error message component is not present, leaving the error undisclosed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the ErrorMessage for the email field renders with the expected ID (e.g., id="newsletter-email-error") and that the FormGroup passes errorId prop. Add in NewsletterForm around line 960:  
  ```tsx
  <FormGroup
    label="Email address"
    htmlFor="newsletter-email"
    errorId="newsletter-email-error"
    errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  >
    …
  </FormGroup>
  ```

issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup renders a <label without htmlFor, so the name input is not programmatically linked to its label.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs missing aria‑invalid when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the inputs do not receive aria‑invalid, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add aria-invalid to each input/textarea, e.g., for the name field:  
  Line 1085: `<TextInput
          id="contact-name"
          name="name"
          type="text"
          autoComplete="name"
          required
          aria-required="true"
+          aria-invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
          … />`  
  Apply similarly to email, subject, and message fields.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm inputs error messages not linked via aria‑describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The aria‑describedby attributes reference IDs that are never rendered because the error messages are not associated with the inputs.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass errorId and errorMessage to FormGroup so the ErrorMessage renders with the correct ID, e.g., for the name field:  
  ```tsx
  <FormGroup
    label="Full name"
    htmlFor="contact-name"
    errorId="contact-name-error"
    errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}
    required
  >
    …
  </FormGroup>
  ```

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px × 28px with padding: 0, which falls below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). Users with motor impairments or those using touch screens may have difficulty activating this button accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 684-693: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below absolute minimum for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit dimensions of 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size Minimum AA). This makes the control difficult to activate for users with limited dexterity or on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1205: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size for footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px, which is between the 24px minimum and 44px recommended size. Without confirmed compensating padding of at least 8px per side, these links may not meet the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8), potentially affecting users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1274: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without non-colour alternative in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation have text-decoration: none applied, and the hover state only changes colour without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border, or background change). issuesFound.title: Toggle button aria-pressed not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button (_playBtn) does not have its aria-pressed state initialised to "false" if the autoplay configuration is disabled. Screen readers will not know the initial state of the control.
issuesFound.fix: Line: testimonialsCarousel.init: Add `if (_playBtn && !_playing) { setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'false'); }` after the autoplay check.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus updates aria-expanded but does not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after setting aria-expanded. Line: dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after setting aria-expanded.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner visibility not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Showing or hiding the cookie consent banner does not announce the change to screen reader users. They may be unaware a dialog has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line: cookieConsent._showBanner: Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences dialog opened');` before focusing. Line: cookieConsent._hideBanner: Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookie preferences dialog closed');` before removing.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the input field does not receive aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line: discountCodeInput._onApply: In the `else` block (invalid result), add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically revealed elements not hidden from accessibility tree initially
issuesFound.explanation: Elements with data-reveal are observed for visibility but are not set to aria-hidden="true" before they are revealed. If hidden via CSS (e.g., opacity), they remain in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line: viewportReveal.init: Before observing, add `qsa(SELECTOR).forEach(el => setAttr(el, 'aria-hidden', 'true'));`. In `_reveal`, add `el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger lacks keyboard activation and role enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation trigger (_trigger) is not checked to ensure it is a native button. If it is a div, it lacks role="button", tabindex="0", and Enter/Space key activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: mobileNav.init: Add check `if (_trigger.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_trigger, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(_trigger, 'tabindex', '0'); }`. Add `if (e.key === KEYS.ENTER || e.key === KEYS.SPACE) { e.preventDefault(); toggle(); }` to _onTriggerKeydown.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger lacks role and tabindex enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger element is not enforced to have role="button" and tabindex="0" if it is not a native button element, potentially making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: dropdownMenu.register: Add `if (trigger.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(trigger, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(trigger, 'tabindex', '0'); }` before adding event listeners.

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button lacks role and keyboard support enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (_btn) is not enforced to be a native button or given role="button" and tabindex="0". It also lacks explicit Enter/Space key handling if it is a div.
issuesFound.fix: Line: scrollToTop.init: Add `if (_btn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_btn, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(_btn, 'tabindex', '0'); }`. Add keydown listener for Enter/Space to trigger _onClick.

issuesFound.title: Search clear button lacks role and keyboard support enforcement
issuesFound.explanation: The search clear button (_clearBtn) is not enforced to be a native button or given role="button" and tabindex="0", risking keyboard inaccessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line: siteSearch.init: Add `if (_clearBtn && _clearBtn.tagName !== 'BUTTON') { setAttr(_clearBtn, 'role', 'button'); setAttr(_clearBtn, 'tabindex', '0'); }`.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent preferences trigger lacks accessibility attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The [data-cookie-prefs] trigger element is not managed for role, tabindex, or aria-expanded state, making it potentially inaccessible and its state unclear.
issuesFound.fix: Line: cookieConsent.init: In _onPrefsClick, ensure target has `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, and toggle `aria-expanded="true/false"` when showing/hiding banner.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu` module functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` update the visibility of dropdown panels and toggle `aria-expanded` on triggers, but they do not announce these state changes to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology will not be informed when a dropdown opens or closes, unlike other modules (e.g., `mobileNav`, `faqAccordion`) which correctly use `liveRegion.announce`.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1635: Add `liveRegion.announce(inst.open ? 'Menu expanded' : 'Menu collapsed');` inside `_openInstance` after setting attributes.
  Line ~1655: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');` inside `_closeInstance` after setting attributes.

issuesFound.title: Stat counter animation updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `statCounterAnimator` module function `_animate` updates the `textContent` of stat elements to count up numbers when they scroll into view. However, the element does not have `aria-live` set, and no live region announcement is made during the animation. Screen reader users will not hear the numbers changing, only the final value if `aria-label` is updated at the end.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~3685: Add `el.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');` at the start of `_animate` function before calling `animateNumber`, or call `liveRegion.announce` in the `onUpdate` callback for significant milestones.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
Stat counter animation updates not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer aria-hidden not synced
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer element (_drawer) has its visibility toggled via CSS classes (addClass/removeClass) in the open and close functions, but the aria-hidden attribute is not explicitly updated to match. While CSS display:none removes elements from the accessibility tree, off-canvas patterns often use transforms which do not, requiring aria-hidden="true" when closed to prevent screen reader access.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1336: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1366: _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu update the DOM and aria-expanded state but do not announce the state change (e.g., "Menu opened") to screen readers via a live region. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the dropdown state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1553: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1572: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: Pricing plan highlight action not announced

issuesFound.fix: Line 3039: liveRegion.announce('Plan highlighted as recommended');

issuesFound.title: Discount code input validation error not reflected in aria-invalid

issuesFound.fix: Line 3199: if (!result.valid) { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play/pause toggle not announced

issuesFound.fix: Line 3870: liveRegion.announce(_playing ? 'Auto-play paused' : 'Auto-play started');

issuesFound.title: File upload field validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _addFile function in fileUploadField announces validation errors via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the native file input (binding.inputEl). This violates WCAG 3.3.1 as the error state is not programmatically determined on the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5063: if (!validation.valid) { binding.inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner trigger aria-expanded not managed
issuesFound.explanation: The _showBanner function sets aria-hidden="false" on the banner but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button ([data-cookie-prefs]). Additionally, the trigger's aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load. This breaks the association between the trigger and the expanded state of the banner.

issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput element references 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but this ID is never rendered in the DOM. This leaves screen reader users without access to field-specific validation error messages.

'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />
  (Note: To fully fix, the FormGroup call must also be updated to pass errorId="newsletter-email-error" and errorMessage={email.state.error}.)

issuesFound.title: Table data cells rely on aria-label for meaning
issuesFound.explanation: The table uses aria-label on elements to convey "Not included" or "Included" (e.g., aria-label="Not included">—). aria-label is not consistently supported on table cells by assistive technologies (JAWS and NVDA typically ignore it), meaning screen reader users may hear only "dash" or silence instead of the intended state. This fails WCAG 1.3.1 Info and Relationships because the information is not programmatically determinable by all assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Filter tabs use toggle button pattern instead of tabs or radio buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The filter controls (All tools, Scanning, Design, Testing, Reporting) function as a single-select tab list (only one can be active at a time) but are implemented as <button> elements with aria-pressed inside a role="group". This does not convey the tab list semantics (role="tablist", role="tab", aria-selected) or radio group semantics, making it difficult for screen reader users to understand that the content below updates when a different filter is selected. This fails WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

Line 371: <div role="tablist" aria-label="Product category filter">

  Line 382: <button type="button" role="tab" aria-selected="false" data-filter="testing">Testing</button>
  Line 385: <button type="button" role="tab" aria-selected="false" data-filter="reporting">Reporting</button>

issuesFound.title: Social media links have redundant accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links have both an aria-label ("Luminary Studio on Twitter (opens in new tab)") and visible text ("Twitter"). The aria-label overrides the visible text, which is acceptable, but the visible text is redundant for screen reader users. However, the visible text is useful for sighted users. This is not a violation but could be optimized by removing aria-label and ensuring the visible text is sufficient, or by using aria-labelledby pointing to the visible text plus additional off-screen text for "opens in new tab".

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets an explicit min-height of 36px (below the 44px recommended minimum) with vertical padding of only 6px (0.375rem), providing less than 8px of compensation. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503: Increase min-height to 44px or add at least 8px vertical padding to ensure the interactive area meets the recommended 44×44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width, height, and min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with tremors or limited dexterity may struggle to activate this control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1400: Increase width, height, and min-height to at least 24px (e.g., width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for semi-transparent text on gradient background
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label uses rgba(255,255,255,0.75) (75% white) text on a gradient background containing #005fcc (blue) and #6b21a8 (purple). Against the blue portion, the blended text color creates a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (14px). Low-vision users will have difficulty reading the labels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 936: Change color to opaque white (#ffffff) or increase opacity to at least 0.9 (rgba(255,255,255,0.9)) and verify contrast ratio exceeds 4.5:1 against the darkest background color.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for semi-transparent paragraph text on gradient background
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section paragraph uses rgba(255,255,255,0.8) text on a gradient background containing #005fcc. The resulting blended color provides approximately 3.5:1 contrast against the blue background, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal 16px text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1356: Change color to rgba(255,255,255,0.95) or #ffffff to ensure at least 4.5:1 contrast ratio against the gradient background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for semi-transparent label text on gradient background
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form labels use rgba(255,255,255,0.9) on the gradient background. Against the #005fcc blue portion, this yields approximately 4.0:1 contrast, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal 14px text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Change color to #ffffff (opaque white) to ensure sufficient contrast against the darkest part of the gradient background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for input hint text on gradient background
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint in the newsletter section uses rgba(255,255,255,0.65), which blends with the gradient background to produce a contrast ratio of approximately 2.5:1 against the blue portion, failing WCAG AA requirements for text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1397: Change color to rgba(255,255,255,0.9) or higher to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast ratio.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for large text indicators in comparison table
issuesFound.explanation: The "Not included" and "Not available" table cells use var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) text at font-size-xl (20px). Against the white background (and especially the neutral-50 zebra-stripe background), this provides only approximately 2.3–2.8:1 contrast, failing the 3:1 requirement for large text (18px+).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1328: Change color to var(--colour-neutral-600) (#475569) or darker to achieve at least 3:1 contrast against both white and neutral-50 backgrounds.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The placeholder text uses var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) on inputs with white or neutral-50 backgrounds. This provides approximately 2.8:1 contrast, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. Users with low vision may be unable to read the placeholder content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 228: Change color to var(--colour-neutral-600) (#475569) or darker to achieve at least 4.5:1 contrast against the input background.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links (nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a) remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change (to --colour-brand-primary) on hover. No border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight is provided. Colour-blind users may not perceive the link state change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: Add border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; and Line 1159: Add border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); to the hover state, OR add text-decoration: underline to the hover state.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: Increase width and height to at least 24px, or add sufficient padding/margin to ensure the interactive area meets 24×24px. Example: `width: 24px; height: 24px;` or wrap with a larger clickable label area.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant has a min-height of 36px (between 24px and 43px) with only 6px vertical padding (var(--space-1-5)), which is below the 8px compensation threshold. While it meets the minimum 24px, it may not meet the recommended 44px touch target size for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 470: Increase min-height to 44px, or add padding to ensure the total interactive height reaches 44px. Example: `min-height: 44px;` or `padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5);` (8px vertical padding).

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient compensation. This is below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: Increase width and height to 44px, or add padding/margin to ensure a 44×44px touch area. Example: `width: 44px; height: 44px;` or add `padding: 8px;` while keeping the visual size 28px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no padding specified, placing them at exactly the minimum size without the recommended 44px touch target. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1093: Increase min-height to 44px, or add padding-block to ensure the interactive area meets 44px. Example: `min-height: 44px; padding-block: 10px;` or `padding: var(--space-3);`.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text (Account navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the default underline with `text-decoration: none` and rely solely on colour change (to brand primary) on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as colour alone is not sufficient to distinguish links from surrounding text for users with colour vision deficiencies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 554: Add a non-colour visual differentiator such as `text-decoration: underline` on hover, or a `border-bottom`, or ensure links are underlined by default. Example addition after line 557: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text (Footer navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links remove underlines with `text-decoration: none` and only change colour on hover (to neutral-0), relying solely on colour to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1094: Add `text-decoration: underline` on hover or a visible border-bottom. Example addition after line 1096: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text (Social links)
issuesFound.explanation: The social media links remove underlines with `text-decoration: none` and rely solely on colour change (to neutral-0) on hover to indicate they are interactive. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as there is no additional visual indicator such as underline or border.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1105: Add `text-decoration: underline` on hover, or add a `border-bottom` property. Example addition after line 1106: `.social-links a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring replacement. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 424-433: Replace with correct visually-hidden technique: position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies transform: translateY(1px) on :active. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 544: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:active { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) and transitions the transform property. Users with motion sensitivity may experience discomfort from this animation, and there is no reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 496: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition and applies transform: translateY(-1px) on hover. No reduced-motion media query disables this spatial movement.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 902: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary, .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .product-card
issuesFound.explanation: Product cards use transform: translateY(-3px) on hover with a transition. This spatial movement is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add after line 1198: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .product-card, .product-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation persists in reduced-motion mode on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule at line 1212 only targets .pricing-card:hover, not this specific modifier, so the animation persists when reduced motion is preferred.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1212: Change selector to .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on secondary text in section headers
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text in .section-header uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 256: Change color to var(--colour-neutral-600) or darker: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card descriptions
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, providing only 2.3:1 contrast, failing WCAG AA for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1024: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on step descriptions
issuesFound.explanation: Step content paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background (from .step background), resulting in 2.3:1 contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1079: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on products section header text
issuesFound.explanation: The section header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, failing the 4.5:1 contrast requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1153: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing section header text
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section header paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white, providing insufficient contrast for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1183: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on testimonial author details
issuesFound.explanation: The figcaption paragraph in testimonial cards uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, resulting in 2.3:1 contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1380: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on contact section description
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section paragraph uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f8fafc), which is close to white, resulting in approximately 2.3:1 contrast.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1436: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hints use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white background, failing the 4.5:1 contrast requirement for small text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1466: Change color: var(--colour-text-secondary); to color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator in account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour change (from neutral-700 to brand-primary) to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing SC 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 786: Add border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; and on hover add border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); or add font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use text-decoration: none with only a colour change on hover (neutral-400 to neutral-0), providing no non-colour visual indicator that these are links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1232: Add text-decoration: underline; on hover, or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator in footer bottom
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom links remove underlines and only show underline on hover, with no other non-colour differentiator (such as font-weight or border) at rest.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: Add border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-neutral-400); or maintain underline: text-decoration: underline;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator in dropdown menus
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links use text-decoration: none and rely only on background-color change on hover, with no non-colour differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) at rest to identify them as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 692: Add border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; and on hover add border-bottom-color: var(--colour-brand-primary);
