issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px, which does not render a visible focus ring. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see which element has focus because outline-offset alone is not visible without an outline width.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the default outline with outline: none and only sets outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This leaves button components without any visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 574: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1238: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, falling within the 24–43px range without sufficient padding to reach the recommended 44px touch target size. This may make activation difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 756: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This completely breaks the intended functionality of screen-reader-only text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies a transform: translateY(1px) spatial movement without a corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override. Users who have opted for reduced motion may still experience this displacement animation, which can cause vestibular discomfort.
issuesFound.fix: Line 576: `.btn:active { transform: translateY(1px); }` and add Line 414: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The h1–h6 heading selector applies letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing reduces the readability of text, particularly for users with low vision or dyslexia, by crowding characters together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: `h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6 { font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold); line-height: var(--line-height-tight); letter-spacing: 0em; color: var(--colour-text-primary); text-wrap: balance; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom, text-decoration on hover, or background highlight. The hover state only changes the colour (line 1255), meaning users who cannot perceive colour differences may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1254: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: underline; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }` or add Line 1255: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring because the outline width is zero. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px. A negative offset on a zero-width outline produces no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color. Without an outline width, the color property creates no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width of 28px and height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which meets the minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended target size, making them harder to activate for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button base class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. While padding may increase the clickable area, the explicit small min-height risks insufficient touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended target size and may be difficult for some users to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transition: transform for its slide-in animation, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset property, which does not render a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot perceive which element currently has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for users in high-contrast mode who rely on clear focus boundaries to navigate.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class only defines outline-offset: -3px without setting an outline style or width, inheriting outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule. This results in no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The rule sets only outline-color without defining outline-style or outline-width. Because the base :focus-visible rule sets outline: none, this combination produces no visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus indicator that keyboard users require to identify the currently focused button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation links and buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible rules for main navigation links and buttons set outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving keyboard users without a visible focus indicator when navigating the menu.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The submenu link focus-visible state removes the outline and provides only a negative outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus ring for keyboard users navigating dropdown menus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, denying keyboard users a visible focus indicator when the button receives focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation link focus-visible state removes the outline and provides only an offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users accessing the account menu.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons reliably.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width and min-height to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes activation difficult for users with tremors or limited motor precision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width and height to 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users with motor disabilities may have difficulty selecting the checkbox accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets an explicit height of 28px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While above the 24px minimum, the small target size may still cause difficulty for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets explicit width and height of 28px, below the 44px recommended size. This small target may be difficult for users with motor impairments to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links set min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended touch target size, potentially causing difficulty for users with limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort or nausea from the movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform in its transition, creating motion effects without a reduced-motion override. This can trigger vestibular issues for sensitive users when the button is hovered or focused.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by reducing the visual separation between words.
issuesFound.fix:


issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and applies a translateY transform on :active, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 419: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 416) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition and applies translateY on hover, but lacks a reduced-motion override.
issuesFound.fix: Line 689: (add to @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img transitions on transform and applies perspective transforms on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1326 is empty and does not disable the animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 744: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: .pricing-card--featured:hover applies translateY and scale transforms, but the reduced-motion media query at line 1152 only targets .pricing-card:hover, missing the featured variant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1158: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 285: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses white text (#ffffff) on a #5588bb background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (12px-18px).
issuesFound.fix: Line 432: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); } (or use a darker background that meets 4.5:1)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on main navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: #nav-menu > li > a sets text-decoration: none and provides only a color change on hover without an underline or other non-colour visual differentiator, making links indistinguishable from text for colour-blind users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 507: #nav-menu > li > a { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: underline; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 28px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a removes underlines and relies solely on colour to distinguish links, failing SC 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); text-decoration: underline; color: var(--colour-neutral-700); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a removes underlines with no alternative visual differentiator, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text except by colour.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below the default. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and those using screen magnification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 189: `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only outline-offset. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring; users navigating by keyboard cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335-338: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a high-contrast alternative such as a visible border or box-shadow. Users in Windows High Contrast mode will lose focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346-349: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus indicator from buttons. Keyboard users cannot visually identify focused buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 422-424: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended target size. Although padding is present, it is only ~7.5px (var(--space-2-5) at 12px root), totalling less than 44px, making activation difficult for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 410: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector includes transform in its transition property (line 426), causing spatial movement on state changes. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 436 is empty and does not disable this transition, which can trigger vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 436: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm modifier sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Small touch targets are difficult for users with limited dexterity to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 474: `min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets both min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are particularly critical for touch accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 485-486: `min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with only 6px padding (var(--space-2)), failing the 44px recommended target size. Mobile navigation toggles must be easily activatable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 558-559: `min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: These navigation elements set min-height: 28px with only 6px vertical padding, resulting in a touch target below the 44px recommendation. Navigation links require adequate sizing for reliable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets explicit height: 28px with 6px padding, resulting in a total height of only 28px (border-box), well below the 44px recommendation. Form inputs need adequate sizing for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible replacement such as box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see a focus ring on any focused element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 287: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 298: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button has transition: transform var(--transition-fast) which causes spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The active state uses transform: translateY(1px) for spatial movement without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses transition: transform var(--transition-base) for its slide-in animation without respecting prefers-reduced-motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button has transition including transform and hover state with translateY(-1px), lacking reduced-motion support.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) and hover transform with perspective rotation, which can trigger vestibular issues without a reduced-motion fallback.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) for spatial movement without a reduced-motion override (the existing @media block only covers .pricing-card, not the featured variant).

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.fix:
Line 268: body { word-spacing: normal; }
  Line 269: body { letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social media links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links in the footer remove the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, leaving keyboard users without focus indication.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1255-1258: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-neutral-0); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size, and has zero padding on those axes, resulting in a touch target of only 28×28 pixels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 546-548: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets an explicit min-height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Although padding is present, the minimum dimension is insufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line 503-509: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button sets explicit min-width and min-height of 20px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While padding increases the clickable area to 40px, this remains below the recommended 44×44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 515-520: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set an explicit min-height of 24px with no vertical padding specified in the same rule, resulting in a touch target of exactly 24px, which meets the minimum but is below the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240-1242: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on form checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1226-1228: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text remains visible in the layout, and the class does not use clip or clip-path to hide content from view while keeping it accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376-387: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (for the :active state translateY effect) but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override is provided to disable this spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 473-476: transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); (remove transform transition)
  Line 406-408: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties (rotateY, rotateX) that creates spatial movement on hover, but the prefers-reduced-motion media query at line 656 is empty and does not disable this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 656-661: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on feature cards
issuesFound.explanation: Feature cards use transform: translateY in their hover transition, but there is no prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 766-768: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card { transition: none; } .feature-card:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation without prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state uses transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), but the existing prefers-reduced-motion block at line 978 only targets the base .pricing-card:hover, missing the featured variant.
issuesFound.fix: Line 978-980: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and reduces readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. Negative word-spacing can cause words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 242-243: word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-text-secondary variable is set to #aaaaaa (light gray), which against a white background (#ffffff) produces a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text. This affects .section-header p and other elements using this color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: --colour-text-secondary: #767676; (or any color that provides 4.5:1 contrast against white)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on step content paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: Step content paragraphs use var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white backgrounds, resulting in a 2.9:1 contrast ratio which is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: --colour-text-secondary: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing period text
issuesFound.explanation: The price period text (e.g., "/month") uses #aaaaaa on white backgrounds in the pricing cards, failing the 4.5:1 contrast requirement for small text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: --colour-text-secondary: #767676;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table "not included" cells
issuesFound.explanation: Table cells indicating "Not included" use var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) on white or light gray backgrounds, resulting in a contrast ratio below 3:1, making it difficult to distinguish from the background.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1089-1093: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use #94a3b8 (var(--colour-neutral-400)) against a #555555 background (var(--colour-neutral-900)), producing approximately 3.1:1 contrast, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240-1242: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: The footer address uses #64748b (var(--colour-neutral-500)) on a #555555 background, resulting in approximately 2.0:1 contrast, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1232-1234: .footer-brand address { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); line-height: var(--line-height-loose); font-style: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom copyright text uses #64748b (var(--colour-neutral-500)) on #555555 background, with approximately 2.0:1 contrast, failing WCAG AA requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244-1246: .footer-bottom > p { font-size: var(--font-size-xs); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); margin: 0; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration: none from all links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator such as border-bottom, font-weight, or background highlight in the default state. Links rely solely on color (#5588bb) to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: a { text-decoration: underline; } or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to the global a selector.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a user activates a dropdown trigger to open or close a menu panel, the visual state change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users receive no feedback that content has appeared or disappeared, leaving them unaware of the expanded or collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2170: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and Line 2185: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria-hidden on background content
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the main page content remains in the accessibility tree because `aria-hidden="true"` is not programmatically applied to the primary content container. Screen reader users can navigate outside the modal drawer context, violating the expected modal behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1755: Add `setAttr(document.getElementById('main-content'), 'aria-hidden', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);` and remove it in the `close` function.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner lacks aria-hidden on background siblings
issuesFound.explanation: The cookie banner is marked as `aria-modal="true"` but the focus trap implementation does not apply `aria-hidden="true"` to sibling elements of the banner. Background content remains accessible to screen readers while the modal is active, creating a confusing experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3058: Before `trap.activate()`, add code to iterate over `_bannerEl.parentElement.children` and set `aria-hidden="true"` on all siblings, storing previous values to restore on close.

issuesFound.title: Focus trap utility missing background aria-hidden management
issuesFound.explanation: The focus trap confines keyboard navigation to the container but fails to remove the rest of the document from the accessibility tree by setting `aria-hidden="true"` on background elements. This is required for proper modal dialog accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Add logic to find all sibling elements of the container and set `aria-hidden="true"`, preserving the container and any existing aria-hidden values to restore on deactivate.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher toggle buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle buttons (`[data-theme-toggle]`) do not have their `aria-pressed` attribute initialized to reflect the current theme state on page load. Screen readers cannot indicate which theme is active until the user interacts with the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3110: After `_apply(_resolve(_current));`, add `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle') === _current)); });`

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggles missing initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: View mode toggle buttons (`[data-view-mode]`) lack the `aria-pressed` attribute on initialization to indicate which view (grid or list) is currently active. The attribute is only set after user interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2460: After applying the view mode class, add `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });`

issuesFound.title: Stat counter value changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When statistic counters animate and update their text content as they enter the viewport, screen reader users are not informed of the final values because no live region announcement is made. The dynamic content change is invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2920: Inside the `onComplete` callback of `animateNumber`, add `liveRegion.announce('Statistic updated: ' + _format(cfg.target, cfg));`

fpTitles: Dropdown menu open/close not announced to screen readers
Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria-hidden on background content
Cookie consent banner lacks aria-hidden on background siblings
Focus trap utility missing background aria-hidden management
Theme switcher toggle buttons missing initial aria-pressed state
Product filter view mode toggles missing initial aria-pressed state
Stat counter value changes not announced

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions lack live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in the dropdownMenu module toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update aria-expanded on the trigger, but they do not announce the state change (opened/closed) to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies will not be notified when a dropdown menu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1743: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened') after setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); Line 1763: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed') after setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render updates lack live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The render function in the activeFilterChips module dynamically adds or removes filter chip elements from the DOM when filter criteria change, but it does not announce these changes to screen readers. Users are not informed when active filters are added or removed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2844: After _containerEl.removeAttribute('hidden'); and after the chips are appended, add: if (chips.length > 0) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filtersUpdated || `Active filters: ${chips.map(c => c.label).join(', ')}`); } else { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filtersCleared || 'All filters cleared'); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The close function in the mobileNav module removes the open CSS class from the drawer element but does not set the hidden HTML attribute or aria-hidden ARIA attribute. If the drawer's default CSS state is not display:none, it will remain in the accessibility tree when closed, allowing screen reader users to navigate to invisible content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1587: In the close function, after removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); add: _drawer.setAttribute('hidden', ''); Line 1543: In the open function, after addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); add: _drawer.removeAttribute('hidden');

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel navigation buttons missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The previous and next buttons in the testimonialsCarousel module control the visibility of slide elements but do not have aria-controls attributes referencing the slide container or individual slides. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the relationship between the buttons and the content they control.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3237: After _prevBtn = qs('[data-carousel-prev]', _rootEl); add: if (_prevBtn && _trackEl) _prevBtn.setAttribute('aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_trackEl, 'carousel-track')); Line 3238: Similarly for _nextBtn.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu toggle functions lack live-region announcement
activeFilterChips render updates lack live-region announcement
mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when closed
testimonialsCarousel navigation buttons missing aria-controls

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hidden state lacks aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header is hidden via the hide() function, it is likely moved off-screen using CSS transforms rather than display:none. The code does not set aria-hidden="true", leaving the header in the accessibility tree and allowing screen-reader users to navigate to an invisible element.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3421: Add `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
    Line 3430: Add `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer lacks aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav drawer is toggled closed using CSS classes. If the CSS uses off-screen positioning (e.g., transform: translateX) instead of display:none, the drawer remains accessible to screen readers. The close() function does not set aria-hidden="true" on the drawer element.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3188: Add `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');`
    Line 3167: Add `_drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input (selector '[name="email"]' mapped to #contact-email) collects personal data but the code does not set the autocomplete attribute. This prevents browsers and assistive technologies from correctly identifying the field's purpose.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5736: After `if (field) inlineValidator.bind(field, cfg.rules);` add:
    `if (cfg.selector.includes('email')) setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form first name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input (selector '[name="full_name"]' or specifically first-name) lacks autocomplete. The id "contact-first-name" signals given-name data.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5736: After binding, add:
    `if (field && field.id === 'contact-first-name') setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'given-name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form last name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The last name input (id "contact-last-name") lacks the autocomplete attribute for family-name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5736: After binding, add:
    `if (field && field.id === 'contact-last-name') setAttr(field, 'autocomplete', 'family-name');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input is queried but autocomplete="email" is never set, preventing proper identification by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5641: Add `setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu hover activation not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens on mouse hover (_onHover), the code does not move focus or announce the state change via a live region. Screen-reader users navigating with a mouse will not be aware the menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3296: Inside the 'enter' block after `_openInstance(inst);`, add:
    `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel play button incorrect initial aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button is initialized with aria-pressed="true" regardless of whether auto-play is actually active. If auto-play is disabled, the button indicates the carousel is playing when it is not.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6120: Change `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` to `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent banner not hidden from accessibility tree during fade out
issuesFound.explanation: The banner is faded out using the fadeOut utility which sets opacity to 0 before removing the element. During the 200ms fade, the banner remains in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden, potentially allowing interaction with invisible controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6510: Before `fadeOut(_bannerEl, ...`, add:
    `setAttr(_bannerEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: Wizard step transition lacks focus fallback
issuesFound.explanation: When advancing steps, focus is moved to the first heading or element with data-wizard-focus. If no such element exists in the step panel, focus is lost to the document body, forcing screen-reader users to navigate from the top of the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5872: After the if-block for firstHeading, add:
    `else { entering.panelEl.setAttribute('tabindex', '-1'); entering.panelEl.focus({ preventScroll: false }); }`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility and CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1317: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the _open = true; assignment.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1447: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after inst.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function animates the panel height and removes the hidden attribute but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". This leaves screen reader users unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2620: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after item.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2040: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); inside the function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open() fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus management but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it in the open() function. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile navigation drawer when open, allowing focus to escape to background elements (WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: Add _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); and Line 1321: Add _trap.activate(); after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open() fails to move focus into navigation drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus remains on the hamburger trigger button instead of moving to the drawer or its first focusable element. Keyboard users must manually navigate into the newly opened content (WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1325: Add code to focus the first focusable element or the drawer itself: const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks live region announcements for open/close
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels in response to user interaction but does not announce these state changes via a live region. Screen reader users receive no feedback when a dropdown expands or collapses (WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1452: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened'); in _openInstance. Line 1474: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed'); in _closeInstance before or alongside the emit call.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-selected
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons use a roving tabindex pattern but the active tab is only indicated via CSS class. The function fails to set aria-selected="true" on the active tab and aria-selected="false" on inactive tabs, preventing screen readers from identifying the selected tab (WCAG 4.1.2).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2368: Add setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive)); inside the forEach loop.

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips focus loss on chip removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip button is clicked and removed from the DOM, focus is lost because the code does not programmatically move focus to the next chip, the container, or any other element. This creates a focus management issue for keyboard users (WCAG 2.4.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2543: Before removing the chip element, determine the next focusable element (next sibling chip or the container) and call .focus() on it, or store a reference to the next chip and focus it after the removal.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect
issuesFound.explanation: The file upload zone sets aria-dropeffect to "copy" and "none". This attribute is deprecated in ARIA 1.1 and removed in ARIA 1.2; modern screen readers may not support it, and it provides no functional accessibility benefit.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3404: Remove setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'copy'); and Line 3414: Remove setAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect', 'none');. The drag-and-drop functionality should be indicated via accessible name and instructions instead.

fpTitles: mobileNav open() fails to move focus into navigation drawer
activeFilterChips focus loss on chip removal
fileUploadField uses deprecated aria-dropeffect

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the function adds CSS classes and emits an event but fails to update the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel and sets internal state but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the code animates the panel and updates classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen reader users unaware that the panel is now open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `item.open = true;`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (per the HTML contract comment referencing aria-pressed) but the synchronization function only toggles CSS classes. It never sets aria-pressed to reflect the active state, failing WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function marks the field invalid via CSS classes and associates an error message using aria-describedby, but it never sets aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (inside else block): `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The function emits a custom event when closing but does not announce the state change via the live region. Users navigating with screen readers are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu reveals content visually but provides no auditory feedback via a live region. Users may be unaware that the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`
  Fix: Add immediately after: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the dropdown removes it from view but does not announce the collapse to screen readers, leaving users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line: `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable and references it in `close()`, but the trap is never instantiated with `createFocusTrap()` nor activated in `open()`. Consequently, focus is not trapped within the drawer while it is open, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.

Line (in init() after _initialised = true): Add: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger, escapeDeactivates: true, onDeactivate: () => close(false) });`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: These functions control dropdown panel visibility but fail to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger when opened and `"false"` when closed. Assistive technologies cannot convey the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2248: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in `_openInstance`.
  Line 2262: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered, the trigger button does not initialize `aria-expanded` to `"false"` to match the initially hidden panel state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2307: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox. When the suggestion list opens or closes, the code does not update `aria-expanded` on the input to reflect the listbox visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2948: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in `_open`.
  Line 2957: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` in `_close`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox input initializes without `aria-expanded="false"`, failing to indicate that the suggestion list is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3040: After `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle button not updating aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle uses `role="switch"` but does not update `aria-checked` to reflect the current state (month vs. year). Screen readers announce it as an unchecked switch regardless of state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3385: Add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year');` in `setPeriod`.
  Line 3425: After setting role="switch" in `init`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', _pricingState.period === 'year');`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the field is styled and linked to an error message via `aria-describedby`, but `aria-invalid` is not set to `"true"`. When valid, `aria-invalid` is not cleared. Assistive technologies cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3874: In the `if (result.valid)` block, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` (or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`).
  Line 3880: In the else block, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid and markValid not handling aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: These utility functions manage error associations but omit setting `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields and clearing it on valid fields, causing inconsistent ARIA state across forms.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1788: In `markInvalid`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.
  Line 1802: In `markValid`, add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` (or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`).

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but there is no announcement to screen readers about the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products").
issuesFound.fix: Line 3145: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(\`\${totalMatches} products match your filters\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates prices visually, but screen reader users receive no confirmation of the change or the new prices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3386: After updating plan cards, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices switched to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the UI context has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2158: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`.
  Line 2174: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus is not announced, so screen reader users may not realize a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2250: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` in `_openInstance`.
  Line 2264: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` in `_closeInstance`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion triggers missing Enter/Space keyboard handling
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers may be non-native buttons (divs with role="button"). The keydown handler captures Arrow keys, Home, and End, but does not handle Enter or Space keys to toggle the accordion, violating keyboard accessibility for role="button" elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3562: Add cases `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that call `toggle(item)` and `e.preventDefault()`.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher toggle elements missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: Theme toggle elements (selected via `[data-theme-toggle]`) may not be native buttons. The code only binds a `click` event listener, so keyboard users cannot operate them with Enter or Space.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4480: Add keydown event listener to handle Enter/Space, or ensure elements are `<button>` tags. If keeping divs, add `el.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); el.click(); } });` for each toggle.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails (size or type), the error is announced via live region, but the file input element does not receive `aria-invalid="true"`, so the error state is not programmatically associated with the field.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4132: In the validation failure block, add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.
  Line 4115: When adding a valid file, add `removeAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users accept or decline cookies, the banner closes but no confirmation is announced to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if their choice was registered.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4380: Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookies accepted');` in `_onAccept`.
  Line 4386: Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookies declined');` in `_onDecline`.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause button updates aria-pressed but carousel state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: While `aria-pressed` is correctly updated, the change between playing and paused states is not announced via a live region. However, since the button itself conveys state via aria-pressed and visual icon, this is a minor issue.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3716: Add `liveRegion.announce('Carousel playing');` in `play`.
  Line 3723: Add `liveRegion.announce('Carousel paused');` in `pause`.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when an error exists, but the FormGroup wrapping it does not receive an errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element with that id is never rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference that leaves screen-reader users unaware of the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line with FormGroup opening tag: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when invalid, but FormGroup is not passed errorId, so the error message element is never rendered in the DOM. This breaks the programmatic association between the input and its error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line with FormGroup for name field: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references aria-describedby="contact-email-error", but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop, preventing the ErrorMessage from rendering with that id. Screen-reader users cannot access the error description.
issuesFound.fix: Line with FormGroup for email field: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput uses aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when validation fails, but the FormGroup component is missing the errorId prop, so the error element is not present in the document.
issuesFound.fix: Line with FormGroup for subject field: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea aria-describedby references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea includes 'contact-message-error' in its aria-describedby list when invalid, but FormGroup does not receive the errorId prop, so the error message element is never created, leaving the reference dangling.
issuesFound.fix: Line with FormGroup for message field: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader actions group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: This div groups multiple interactive controls (SearchInput, ThemeToggle, CTAs, and mobile toggle) but lacks an aria-label. Screen-reader users navigating by landmark or group will encounter an unnamed group of controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: <div className="site-header__actions" aria-label="Header actions">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav footer CTAs group missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The footer contains two call-to-action links ("Log in" and "Get started free") but the div wrapping them has no aria-label to identify the group purpose, making it harder for screen-reader users to understand the context of these actions.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: <div className="mobile-nav__footer" aria-label="Account actions">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badge star rating missing role img
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating is conveyed via aria-label on a span, but without role="img" screen-readers may not treat it as a single image entity, potentially causing inconsistent announcement of the star character and text separately.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: <span className="trust-badge" role="img" aria-label="4.9 out of 5 stars on G2">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the outer overlay div instead of the inner dialog div. Screen readers may not correctly identify the modal boundary, causing users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup prop mismatch breaks error association
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component expects errorId and errorMessage props to render an ErrorMessage and associate it via aria-describedby, but NewsletterForm passes a single error prop. This prevents error messages from rendering in the DOM while the input still references a non-existent ID in aria-describedby, leaving screen reader users unaware of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1314: <FormGroup
  label="Email address"
  htmlFor="newsletter-email"
  errorId="newsletter-email-error"
  errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}
  required
>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm server error message not rendered correctly

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field hint text not displayed

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div interactive without button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay div has an onClick handler to close the modal but lacks role="button", tabIndex={0}, and keyboard event handlers. Keyboard-only users cannot activate this element to close the modal by clicking the backdrop.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1562: Replace `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}>` with `<div className="modal-overlay" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}>` (remove aria-modal from overlay and place on dialog), or add `role="button" tabIndex={0}` and `onKeyDown={(e) => e.key === 'Enter' && onClose()}` to make it keyboard accessible.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid on the underlying input element, preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid propagation
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop but does not map it to aria-invalid on the underlying textarea element, preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal attribute

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog aria-modal attribute misplaced

Line 1 (remove from): <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}>
  Line 2 (add to): <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating widget container missing role="img"
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating widget is a graphical representation of a value but lacks the img role. While it has an aria-label, the absence of role="img" prevents some assistive technologies from treating it as a single image entity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 830: Add `role="img"` to the outer `<span>` element in StarRating.

issuesFound.title: StarRating individual star icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star characters (★ and ☆) inside the rating widget are exposed as separate text nodes to assistive technology, creating a poor experience when the container already provides the rating value via aria-label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 835: Add `aria-hidden="true"` to each `<span>` element inside the stars.map() callback in StarRating.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk (*) marking required fields is announced by screen readers as "star" before the "(required)" text, creating redundant and potentially confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 905: Change ` *` to ` *`.
